title: state existing next_action program autopilot evidence tier
purpose: H1.1 defines state existing next action program autopilot evidence tier for the agent-driven expert system. Persistence — journal, state.json, graphs, evidence.
scope: Owns `H1.1` only; siblings under `H1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §10 — Branch H — Persistence & state plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#10-branch-h-persistence-state-plane).
behavior_steps: On every pursuit wake, S0 reads top-level `next_action`, `program`, `autopilot`, `evidence_required`, `model_tier`, and `spawn_workers` from `journal/state.json` before the conductor interprets `journal/progress.md` ([B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)).
`route-tier.py --apply` and `validate-workflow.py` consume these legacy router fields to select pipeline phase, model tier, and worker spawn policy without re-deriving state in the LLM.

Additive v2 blocks (`goal`, `platform`, `pursuit`, `hitl`, `company`) extend the router atomically via journal-keeper and `sync-state.py` while preserving existing consumer keys per [APP-B](APP-B-state-json-sketch.md).
When `autopilot.active` is true, `next_action` advances only through S0 apply paths—manual journal edits without dual-write do not change the machine router.
Corrupt or partial `state.json` triggers [A4.4](A4.4-stop-integrity-validate-workflow-state-corrupt.md) integrity stop with H2; pursuit does not resume until validate-workflow passes.

Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `H1.1` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `H1.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `H1.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `H1.1` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.
Create or extend S0 script if behavior is file-derived.
Add unit test under tests/unit/test_h1_1.py when script exists.
Validate `H1.1` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `H1.1` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `H1.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: H1.1 defines state existing next action program autopilot evidence tier for the agent-driven expert system. Persistence — journal, state.json, graphs, evidence. In practice, the system on every pursuit wake, s0 reads top-level `next_action`, `program`, `autopilot`, `evidence_required`, `model_tier`, and `spawn_workers` from `journal/state.json` before the conductor interprets `journal/progress.md` ([b1.1](b1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)), then `route-tier.py --apply` and `validate-workflow.py` consume these legacy router fields to select pipeline phase, model tier, and worker spawn policy without re-deriving state in the llm.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-h 2026-06-28 -->

The v2 machine router in `journal/state.json` carries legacy pursuit fields—`next_action`, `program`, `autopilot`, `evidence_required`, `model_tier`, `spawn_workers`—that route-tier.py and validate-workflow.py read every turn. These top-level keys remain the conductor's first S0 lookup before any LLM interprets progress.

Additive v2 blocks (goal, platform, pursuit, hitl, company) extend without breaking existing consumers; sync-state.py and journal-keeper dual-write both files atomically where possible. Corrupt or partial state triggers [A4.4](A4.4-stop-integrity-validate-workflow-state-corrupt.md) stop. See [APP-B-state-json-sketch](APP-B-state-json-sketch.md) and [Vision §10](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#10-branch-h-persistence-state-plane).
